metadata.title: Land Subsidence in Iran Estimated from a Nationwide InSAR Analysis of Sentinel-1 Observations 2014-2020
metadata.description:
Overview

This dataset is a supplementary material to the paper "Haghighi and Motagh, 2024. Uncovering the Impacts of Depleting Aquifers: A Remote Sensing Analysis of Land Subsidence in Iran, Science Advances". It provides detailed insights into land subsidence across Iran, derived from Sentinel-1 InSAR observations. This dataset is intended for use by researchers, policymakers, and practitioners interested in land subsidence, groundwater depletion, and related fields.

Dataset Contents

  1. Iran_subsidence_rate_2014-2020_Sentinel-1_InSAR_desc_v1.0.0.tif
    Annual rate of land subsidence in Iran over the six-year period, projected from satellite Line of Sight to vertical.
  2. Iran_subsidence_rate_2014-2020_Sentinel-1_InSAR_desc_v1.0.0.jpg
    Subsidence map of Iran visualized as jpg
  3. Iran_subsidence_seasonal_amplitude_2014-2020_Sentinel-1_InSAR_desc_v1.0.0.tif
    Amplitude of seasonal ground deformation, projected from satellite Line of Sight to vertical.
  4. Iran_subsidence_mask_2014-2020_Sentinel-1_InSAR_desc_v1.0.0.tif
    Land subsidence mask, based on the annual rate of land subsidence.

Methodology

The data were derived using Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) analysis of Sentinel-1 satellite imagery. The original SAR data includes more than 6000 scenes of Sentinel-1 images collected across 10 descending tracks between 2014 and 2020. The details can be found in the original paper.

Acknowledgements

We acknowledge the European Space Agency (ESA) for providing the Sentinel-1 satellite data used in this analysis.

License

This dataset is shared under CC BY 4.0 license, which allows for reuse and distribution, provided that the original authors and source are credited.

Citation

Please cite the following if you use this dataset:

  1. Haghighi and Motagh, 2024. Uncovering the Impacts of Depleting Aquifers: A Remote Sensing Analysis of Land Subsidence in Iran, Science Advances.
  2. Haghighi and Motagh, 2024. Land Subsidence in Iran Estimated from a Nationwide InSAR Analysis of Sentinel-1 Observations 2014-2020. Zenodo. doi:10.5281/zenodo.10815578
  3. The dataset contains modified Copernicus Sentinel data 2014-2020, processed by ESA.

Contact

Please contact Mahmud Haghighi for inquiries related to this dataset.
https://www.ipi.uni-hannover.de/en/haghighi
